{{Quote|Lanni had a Pilot's Guild book and flew a lot of work runs, surface-to-surface stuff. Somewhere she had picked up a log-recorder, one of the ancient disk types. No ship has used one in centuries. She couldn't read the characters, of course, but there was a figure most beings in this part of space know, the Queen of Ranroon.|Alexsandr Badure|<ref name="hll" />}}
'''Lanni Troujow''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who, while [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] a surface-to-surface run for a [[Mining/Legends|mining]] outfit on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dellalt/Legends|Dellalt]], found the [[log-recorder]] of the ancient lost [[treasure ship]] ''[[Queen of Ranroon/Legends|Queen of Ranroon]]''. Although Troujow was [[Death/Legends|killed]] before she could hunt for the treasure in earnest, her discovery allowed her sister [[Hasti Troujow|Hasti]] and father figure [[Alexsandr Badure]] to find the fabled prize themselves.

==Biography==
{{Quote|The mining camp lies on the far side of the mountains, Han, but I'd reckoned we'd go around. Why should Lanni have been interested in them, I wonder?|Alexsandr Badure|<ref name="hll" />}}
Orphaned at an early age, Lanni Troujow grew up with her sister [[Hasti Troujow|Hasti]] in the [[Tion Hegemony/Legends|Tion Hegemony]], eking out a meager life working at a variety of [[Mining/Legends|mines]] and camps. Having inherited a [[Pilot's Guild]] book from her father, Lanni was able to bolster their income by making surface-to-surface work runs. This practice continued when the Troujow sisters began work as contract labor with an illegal mining outfit on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dellalt/Legends|Dellalt]], where they formed a tight bond with [[Alexsandr Badure]], a former [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] officer who looked after the two sisters in the chaos of the camp. During one of her runs, Troujow happened across an ancient [[log-recorder]] that appeared to be for the ''[[Queen of Ranroon/Legends|Queen of Ranroon]]'', the fabled [[treasure ship]] of the [[Pre-Republic era|pre-Republic]] [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] despot [[Xim/Legends|Xim]], which was bound for Dellalt before its mysterious disappearance.<ref name="hll" />

The log-recorder promised to be the key towards a discovery that could lift the Troujows and Badure out of poverty. Initially, Lanni attempted to investigate the treasure's location on her own, taking quick diversions off of work runs to search promising locations high in the Dellalt [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]]. It was a hunt she never completed—the leader of the gang that ran the mining outfit, [[J'uoch]], had her own designs on the treasure, and murdered Lanni in her own hunt for the log-recorder. However, before her [[Death/Legends|death]], Lanni had managed to secret the log-recorder away in Xim's [[Treasure Vaults of Xim the Despots|treasure vault complex]], keeping the artifact from J'uoch's hands. That allowed Hasti and Badure, who had narrowly escaped Dellalt ahead of J'uoch's vengeance, a shot at the treasure themselves. Returning to Dellalt with [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], and [[Ruurian/Legends|Ruurian]] scholar [[S. V. Skynx/Legends|S. V. Skynx]], Troujow's sister and father figure tracked the ''Queen of Ranroon''{{'s}} treasure down, finishing what she had started.<ref name="hll" />

==Personality and traits==
Along with her sister, Lanni Troujow shared an extremely close bond with Alexsandr Badure, who served as the young women's father figure and protector in the rough Dellalt mining camp. A shrewd investigator and a capable [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], Troujow held a strong resemblance to her sister Hasti, with pale skin and [[Color/Legends|red]] hair.<ref name="hll" />

==Behind the scenes==
Lanni Troujow was first mentioned in ''[[Han Solo and the Lost Legacy]]'', a novel written by [[Brian Daley]] and released in [[1980]].<ref name="hll" />
